objective: Predict student salary packages (in LPA) based on CGPA scores - perfect dataset for practicing linear regression and understanding correlation between academic performance and career outcomes
sources: Student placement records - Pranshu Shah compilation
features_description: Simple two-column dataset: CGPA (out of 10) as predictor and Package (in LPA - Lakhs Per Annum) as target variable for salary prediction
representativity_description: Small collection of 200 student placement records showing relationship between academic performance and starting salaries - limited scope but educational value
sample_balance_description: Good distribution across CGPA range (5.01-9.97) and salary packages (18.7-81.1 LPA)

missing_values.description: Complete dataset with no missing values - ready for immediate analysis

learning_objectives: Understanding correlation between academic performance and salary

salary_distribution.notes: Entry-level salaries in Indian tech industry

data_characteristics.relationship: Generally positive correlation between CGPA and package, with some outliers showing high packages despite lower CGPA

column_details.description: Cumulative Grade Point Average on 10-point scale - primary predictor variable
Salary package in LPA (Lakhs Per Annum) - target variable for regression

technical_specifications.outliers: Some students with lower CGPA achieved higher packages

usage_notes: This is an ideal beginner dataset for linear regression practice. The relationship between CGPA and salary package is generally positive but includes interesting outliers that demonstrate real-world complexity in career outcomes.
potential_applications: Correlation analysis between academic performance and salary
Data visualization practice (scatter plots, regression lines)
Career counseling and salary expectation modeling
analytical_insights: Positive correlation between CGPA and starting salary
CGPA explains significant variance in salary packages
Some high-performers (CGPA 9+) secure premium packages (65+ LPA)
Outliers exist: some students with lower CGPA (6-7) achieve good packages (35+ LPA)
Entry-level tech salaries range widely (18-81 LPA)
Academic performance is important but not the sole determinant of salary
educational_value: Perfect for teaching linear regression concepts
limitations: Limited to specific context (likely Indian tech industry)
Only two variables - lacks additional predictive features
No information about universities, specializations, or companies
Potential sampling bias in data collection

indian_context.salary_context: LPA format common in Indian job market
